name: UniFi Network MCP Server
what_it_does: UniFi Network MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Ubiquiti UniFi network infrastructure — querying connected clients and devices, monitoring network traffic and health, managing SSID configurations, and integrating UniFi's network management platform into agent-driven network administration and homelab automation workflows.
use_cases: Monitoring connected devices and network clients from network management agents
Checking network health and device status from monitoring agents
Managing WiFi configurations and SSIDs from network admin agents
Tracking network bandwidth and traffic from capacity planning agents
Diagnosing network connectivity issues from troubleshooting agents
Automating UniFi network management tasks from homelab agents
not_for: Non-UniFi networks (Cisco, Meraki, Mikrotik require different MCPs)
High-security production changes without review (network changes affect connectivity)
Teams without UniFi hardware and UniFi Network Application
best_when: An agent needs to monitor or manage Ubiquiti UniFi network infrastructure — for homelab management, network monitoring, or network admin automation with UniFi gear.
avoid_when: You're using Cisco, Meraki, or other network vendors — or don't have UniFi hardware.

auth.notes: UniFi Network Application admin username and password required. UniFi Controller URL + credentials. Admin or limited read-only user recommended.

pricing.free_tier_limits: UniFi Network Application: free software (hardware costs separate)
pricing.paid_tiers: UniFi hardware varies; UniFi Cloud Key/Gateways from $79+
pricing.notes: UniFi Network Application software is free. Hardware costs apply for UniFi equipment. Community MCP server is free.

performance.rate_limits: Local UniFi controller — no external rate limits

known_agent_gotchas: UniFi API is unofficial and undocumented — changes with UniFi Network Application updates
Site name is required for multi-site UniFi setups
TLS certificate validation may fail with self-signed UniFi certificates — may need to disable verification
Write operations (config changes) affect live network — test with read-only user first
Community MCP (daqo) — UniFi API changes can break this without warning
UniFi Network Application version affects API compatibility — newer versions may break older MCPs
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local/LAN UniFi controller. Admin credentials — use read-only user for monitoring. Community MCP. Unofficial UniFi API. TLS may have self-signed cert — handle carefully. Network change operations have real impact.

security_score_components.security_notes: LAN access. Use read-only user. Unofficial API. Community MCP. Network changes have real impact.
